figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Top view and (b) side view of the Janus monolayer Cr2SSe structure. Pink, green, and cyan spheres represent Cr, Se, and S atoms, respectively. (c) Phonon dispersion curves. (d) AIMD simulation over 5 ps. (e) Band structure without SOC. (f) Band structure including SOC.

FIG. 2. Band structure and valley polarization of Janus monolayer Cr2SSe under +2% uniaxial strain applied along the x- and y-directions. (a, c) Results for strain along the x-direction; (b, d) results for strain along the y-direction. In the valley polarization diagrams, red and blue cones denote spin-up and spin-down bands, respectively, for the conduction and valence bands. (e, f) Valley polarization as a function of strain along the x- and y-directions, respectively.

FIG. 3. (a) Net magnetic moment of Janus monolayer Cr2SSe as a function of uniaxial strain. (b) Bader charge variation on Cr atoms under uniaxial strain. (c) Bandgap evolution under uniaxial strain. (d) Electric polarization as a function of uniaxial strain.

FIG. 4. Band structure, Berry curvature, valley polarization, and AVHE of Janus monolayer Cr2SSe under uniaxial strain of -2% and -3% applied along the x-direction. (a, c, e) Results for -2% uniaxial strain; (b, d, f) results for -3% uniaxial strain. In the AVHE schematics, arrows indicate spin orientation, with holes and electrons represented by “+” and “–”, respectively.
